channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SEC just hit four companies with big fines for downplaying the SolarWinds hack
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission fined four companies on Tuesday with misleading investors about the impact the 2020 hack of SolarWinds had on their own systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta Oversight Board seriously concerned about Facebook’s ‘overenforcement’ of Harris-Walz nipple post
item.description: Meta Platforms’ Oversight Board on Wednesday told the company to keep up a Facebook post superimposing the faces of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Gartner: 13 AI insights for enterprise IT
item.description: Artificial intelligence is an early stage technology and the hype around it is palpable, but IT leaders need to take many challenges into consideration before making major commitments for their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This viral Tim Walz content was created by Russia, U.S. intelligence confirms
item.description: Groups in Russia created and helped spread viral disinformation targeting Democratic vice presidential candidate Tim Walz, a senior U.S. intelligence official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Why enterprises should care more about net neutrality
item.description: Net neutrality is a topic that arouses nearly as many people as politics does, in no small part because it’s been a political issue. But nearly all of that emotion is at the consumer level. CIOs and network operations and planning professionals cite the topic as important in less than 5% of cases.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents increase their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Parry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The clever tech powering a wave of pig-butchering scams
item.description: Pig-butchering scams are all over social media platforms and are becoming more sophisticated by the day; here's how to spot the latest tricks so you don't become the next victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IBM launches platform to protect data from AI and quantum risks
item.description: IBM has rolled out a new platform to help enterprise customers better handle the complicated data workflows and security risks that arise when managing AI deployments, hybrid cloud infrastructure, and quantum computing systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here’s how to stay safe from scams
item.description: As scammers find new ways to steal money and personal information, consumers should be more vigilant about who they trust, especially online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Flying air taxis move closer to US takeoff with issuing of FAA rule
item.description: Federal regulators are giving a strong push to electric-powered air taxis by issuing a final rule for operating the aircraft and how pilots will be trained to fly them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Russia is behind viral disinformation targeting Walz, intelligence official says
item.description: A senior U.S. intelligence official says viral online content spreading baseless attacks on Democratic vice presidential nominee Tim Walz was created by Russia.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI dominates Gartner’s 2025 predictions
item.description: Artificial Intelligence continues to dominate this week’s Gartner IT Symposium/Xpo, as well as the research firm’s annual predictions list.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk's Government Role Promises Put to Test
item.description: Analysis of Elon Musk's government role promises and his business record
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel, AMD forge x86 alliance
item.description: The old adage “the enemy of my enemy is my ally” has been borne out again, with AMD and Intel joining forces with a number of their largest partners to create the x86 Ecosystem Advisory Group, a consortium that aims to drive forward the ecosystem around x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Wall Street Journal, New York Post sue AI startup
item.description: Media conglomerate News Corp, which owns The Wall Street Journal, New York Post and a number of other media properties, is suing artificial intelligence (AI) startup Perplexity over what it alleges is copyright infringement.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Biden administration awards $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for Michigan factory
item.description: The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Michigan secretary of state pushes back against Musk's voter registration claims
item.description: Michigan Secretary of State Jocelyn Benson (D) and tech billionaire Elon Musk got into a days-long back-and-forth on the social platform X about voter registration in the Wolverine State.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Buyer’s guide: Edge data centers
item.description: Edge data centers include hardware, software, applications, data management, connectivity, gateways, security, and advanced analytics. They come in all shapes and sizes depending on the use case: Small ones can fit into a wiring closet. Larger ones resemble traditional data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Easy ways to make calls when vision is a challenge
item.description: For those with visual challenges, there are a number of voice-dialing apps and features to help make phone calls without having to see numbers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
